notes: Claude local memory is treated as user-local even when exported for review.
MEMORY.md is an index, so topic files carry most durable signal.

records.rationale: Portable rules promote into canonical procedures/behavior/.

metadata.notes: CLAUDE.md is usually a concise shell around imported guidance.

records.content:
# CLAUDE.md

See the following OAC-managed files for context:
- `.oac/claude/project-guidance.md` (Shared guidance)
- `.oac/claude/persona.md` (Agent Persona)
- `.oac/claude/user-model.md` (User Model)

- Promote durable learnings back into the capsule.
- Capsule: `hello-capsule`

records.rationale: User-local material is not promoted by default.
User-local material is not promoted by default.

metadata.notes: Topic files are machine-local by default and should promote deliberately.

records.content:
# architecture

- The canonical artifact is an Open Agent Capsule.
- Adapter profiles customize harness-specific surfaces without changing canonical meaning.
- MCP is an access surface, not the canonical storage layer.

records.rationale: User-local material is not promoted by default.

metadata.notes: Topic files are machine-local by default and should promote deliberately.

records.content:
# preferences

- Prefer file-first architecture.
- Prefer deterministic tests over vague scaffolding.
- Keep launch language honest about what is implemented versus scaffolded.
